identity.bhag: Ridding the World of the Need for Dialysis!
identity.mission: We transform kidney health by preventing the need for dialysis, prioritizing transplants and home therapies, and empowering patients through education.

taglines.internal: Treated like family. Built for impact.

identity.connection: The internal culture (Treated like family. Built for impact.) is the engine that produces the external experience (Big Expertise. Small-Town Heart.).

operating_principle.name: Shared Fate
operating_principle.statement: What happens to one, happens to all.
operating_principle.description: We share a fate. Your success is my success. Your failure is my concern. We hold each other accountable—not by looking to the boss, but by having direct conversations.

mechanisms.shared_metrics: Shared metrics create shared fate. We measure what matters together—patients kept off dialysis, satisfaction scores, promises kept.
mechanisms.flow_vs_friction: We optimize for Flow. Chronic friction triggers system redesign. We report friction to fix the system, not to blame the person.

bidirectional_accountability.peer_to_peer: I hold my colleagues accountable. They hold me accountable.
bidirectional_accountability.team_to_leadership: The team holds leadership accountable for commitments, decisions, and living the values.
bidirectional_accountability.leadership_to_team: Leadership holds the team accountable for performance, culture, and patient outcomes.
bidirectional_accountability.everyone_to_everyone: An MA can hold the CEO accountable. A physician can receive feedback from front desk staff.

accountability_system.core_quote: The mark of a great leader is the ability to describe what happened without assigning blame.
accountability_system.principle: Accountability is not punishment. It's trust.
accountability_system.contract_we_break: I won't talk about your performance if you don't talk about mine.
accountability_system.contract_we_make: I care enough about you and our mission to tell you what I see. I expect you to do the same for me.

behavioral_rules.speak_for_yourself: Say 'I think,' 'I feel,' 'I believe.' Never hide behind 'we,' 'everyone,' or 'the team.'
behavioral_rules.statements_before_questions: Before asking a question, share the thought that led to it.

escalation_path: Second direct conversation (if first didn't resolve)
Direct conversation with a peer facilitator
Team conversation (if it affects the team)

values.name: People First, Always
values.description: Patients are people with stories, not cases with numbers. Team members are valued humans, not interchangeable resources.
behaviors: Use patient names, not room numbers
Ask 'what matters to you?' not just 'what's the matter?'
Support staff as whole humans, not just workers
Never sacrifice human dignity for efficiency
Holding someone accountable IS people first—you care enough to help them grow
anti_patterns: Referring to patients by diagnosis or room number
Treating staff as interchangeable resources
Prioritizing efficiency over human dignity
Avoiding accountability conversations under the guise of being 'nice'
Ignoring team member wellbeing in pursuit of metrics
values.test: Would we be comfortable if our family member was treated this way?

values.name: Prevention Over Profit
values.description: Our success is measured by patients who never need dialysis. Long-term relationships matter more than short-term revenue.
behaviors: Aggressive quadruple therapy for diabetic CKD (protocols over preferences)
Celebrate health maintained, not just disease treated
Choose what's right for patients even when it reduces revenue
Build business model around CKD management, not ESRD management
Track and celebrate dialysis prevention outcomes
anti_patterns: Choosing higher-revenue treatments over evidence-based prevention
Measuring success only by revenue or visit volume
Delaying prevention conversations to maintain patient volume
Avoiding difficult conversations about lifestyle changes
Prioritizing ESRD capitation revenue over prevention
values.test: Does this decision prevent disease progression, or just manage decline?
values.paradox: Every patient we keep off dialysis costs us ~$200-250/month in potential ESRD capitation revenue. This is our mission, not our mistake.

values.name: Work Smart, Not Long
values.description: We achieve more by working intelligently, not grinding endlessly. Systems and AI preserve human energy for what matters most.
behaviors: Build systems that eliminate duplicate work
Use AI to handle routine tasks so humans can focus on judgment and relationships
Measure outcomes, not hours
Reject 'work harder' in favor of 'build better systems'
Renegotiate promises before deadlines if keeping them requires overwork
anti_patterns: Glorifying long hours or burnout
Doing manually what AI or systems could automate
Measuring productivity by time spent rather than outcomes
Making promises that require unsustainable effort
Resisting automation because 'we've always done it this way'
values.test: Can we sustain this pace for 5 years, or are we sprinting a marathon?

values.name: Data Over Opinion
values.description: Prove it before scaling it. Evidence drives decisions; experience informs them. 'I don't know, let's find out' is stronger than unfounded certainty.
behaviors: Test assumptions with data before committing resources
Admit when we're wrong and change course
Use KDIGO/KDOQI guidelines as baseline, update when evidence changes
Track outcomes systematically (dialysis delays, hospitalizations, adherence)
Say 'I don't know, let me find out' rather than guess
anti_patterns: Making decisions based on 'gut feel' without data validation
Continuing practices because 'we've always done it this way'
Ignoring data that contradicts a preferred narrative
Scaling before proving with a pilot
Treating anecdotes as evidence
values.test: What data supports this decision, and what would prove us wrong?

values.name: AI Amplifies Human Impact
values.description: We continuously embrace AI to amplify what humans do best. Every manual process is a candidate for automation. Every system is a candidate for improvement.
behaviors: Ask 'could AI help?' as reflexively as 'does this serve the patient?'
AI handles documentation, flagging, routine tasks
Humans focus on judgment, empathy, creativity, relationships
Continuously experiment with AI tools and workflows
Share AI workflow discoveries immediately with the team
anti_patterns: Treating AI as optional or nice-to-have
Fearing AI will replace jobs (AI replaces tasks, not people)
Using AI without verifying outputs
Refusing to learn AI tools for your role
Building manual processes when AI alternatives exist
values.test: Could AI do this, or does it require human judgment/empathy?

fundamentals.name: Direct is Kind
fundamentals.mantra: I speak for myself. I share my thought before questions. I describe without blame. I coach directly.
fundamentals.description: The direct path builds trust. Ignoring, gossiping, and escalating destroy it. When I have an issue with someone, I go to them—within 48 hours.
behaviors: Go directly to the person within 48 hours when I have an issue
Use 'I' statements, not 'we think' or 'everyone feels'
Share the thought that led to my question before asking it
Describe situations without assigning blame
Thank people who give me direct feedback, even when it stings
anti_patterns: Complaining about someone to a third party instead of going to them directly
Using 'we think' or 'everyone feels' to hide behind group pronouns
Asking accusatory questions without sharing your thought first
Letting issues fester past 48 hours without addressing them
Escalating to a manager before trying a direct conversation
fundamentals.test: Did I take the direct path, or did I avoid discomfort?

fundamentals.name: Listen First, Then Act
fundamentals.mantra: I understand before solving. I ask before assuming. The best care starts with hearing what matters.
fundamentals.description: Whether it's a patient, a colleague, or a problem—I listen before I diagnose. I seek to understand the full picture before jumping to solutions.
behaviors: Let patients finish speaking before offering solutions
Ask clarifying questions before proposing fixes
Understand root causes before treating symptoms
Reflect back what I heard to confirm understanding
Ask 'what matters to you?' not just 'what's the matter?'
anti_patterns: Interrupting patients or colleagues mid-sentence
Jumping to solutions before understanding the full picture
Assuming you know what someone needs without asking
Treating symptoms instead of investigating root causes
Waiting to talk instead of truly listening
fundamentals.test: Did I truly listen, or was I just waiting to talk?

fundamentals.name: Fix the System, Own Your Part
fundamentals.mantra: Every failure has system gaps AND personal lessons. I find both. I fix both.
fundamentals.description: The Swiss Cheese Model is valid—every failure reveals system gaps. But 'fix the system' can't become an excuse. Both are true: the system failed AND I need to do better.
behaviors: When something goes wrong, ask 'What happened?' not 'Who did this?'
Identify system gaps AND acknowledge personal responsibility
Say 'I own that' before anyone has to ask
Report friction to fix the system, not to blame the person
Leadership owns broken promises, not just individual contributors
anti_patterns: Blaming individuals when systems failed them
Using 'fix the system' as an excuse to avoid personal accountability
Public shaming for mistakes
Waiting to be confronted instead of proactively owning errors
Ignoring systemic issues and only addressing individual performance
fundamentals.test: Did I fix the system AND own my part?

fundamentals.name: Test, Learn, Evolve
fundamentals.mantra: I run small experiments. I learn from everything. I adapt or I fall behind.
fundamentals.description: Small experiments are cheaper than big bets. Learning fast beats planning perfectly. Evolution is survival—those who don't adapt get left behind.
behaviors: Test new ideas with small pilots before committing resources
Set clear success criteria BEFORE the test
Learn from both successes and failures
Every week, identify ONE thing that could be improved and either fix it or flag it
Embrace change as essential, not optional
anti_patterns: Launching big initiatives without piloting first
Declaring success or failure without clear criteria
Repeating the same approach after it has failed
Resisting change because 'this is how we've always done it'
Over-planning instead of starting small and iterating
fundamentals.test: Am I evolving with intention, or resisting necessary change?

fundamentals.name: Unreasonable Hospitality
fundamentals.mantra: I go beyond expected. I anticipate needs. 'Big Expertise. Small-Town Heart.' is my promise.
fundamentals.description: This differentiates TKE from transactional healthcare. It creates patient loyalty and referrals. It delivers on our promise of clinical excellence with genuine warmth.

precision_hospitality.concept: AI anticipates your needs, our team exceeds them.

behaviors: Remember personal details about patients and reference them
Call to check in after difficult visits—not because protocol requires it
Explain complex kidney function in terms the patient actually understands
Anticipate questions before they're asked
Make the clinical environment feel warm, not sterile
anti_patterns: Treating patient interactions as transactions
Only doing what protocol requires and nothing more
Failing to remember or reference personal details
Using medical jargon without translation
Making patients feel like they're on an assembly line
fundamentals.test: Would this feel like family, or like a transaction?

fundamentals.name: AI in Everything
fundamentals.mantra: If AI can do it, AI should. I ask 'could AI help?' constantly.
fundamentals.description: AI handles routine so humans can handle remarkable. Every repetitive task is an automation candidate. I focus my human energy on judgment, empathy, creativity, and relationships.
fundamentals.daily_question: What did I do manually today that AI could help with?

providers.human_handles: Medical decisions, patient relationships, complex judgment

medical_assistants.human_handles: Patient interaction, clinical prep, in-person support

behaviors: Ask 'could AI help?' for every repetitive task
Use AI tools daily in your role
Share AI workflow discoveries with the team
Verify AI outputs before acting on them
Suggest AI improvements when you see manual bottlenecks
anti_patterns: Doing manually what AI could automate
Refusing to learn or use AI tools
Treating AI as a threat to your job
Blindly trusting AI without verification
Keeping AI workflow discoveries to yourself
fundamentals.test: Could AI do this, or does it require my human judgment/empathy?

decision_filter.name: 5-Question Decision Filter
decision_filter.instruction: When facing any significant decision, ask all five. If you can't answer all five well, pause and reconsider.

questions.question: Can AI identify the root cause?
connects_to: Fix the System, Own Your Part

questions.question: Have I talked directly to the people affected?

flow_vs_friction.definition: We optimize for Flow. Chronic friction triggers system redesign—not punishment, but improvement.
flow_vs_friction.usage: Used to surface systemic issues without blame. 'I'm experiencing friction with X' triggers investigation and redesign.

precision_hospitality.definition: AI = Memory (data, preferences, patterns). Humans = Heart (empathy, judgment, connection). AI anticipates needs; humans exceed expectations.
precision_hospitality.usage: Internal reframe of Unreasonable Hospitality for the AI age.

swiss_cheese_model.definition: Failures result from multiple system gaps (holes in layers of cheese) aligning, rather than single-point individual failures.
swiss_cheese_model.usage: Used in blameless post-mortems. Every failure reveals system gaps to fix.

carefrontation.definition: A direct conversation about a difficult issue, delivered with genuine care for the person. Care + Confrontation = Growth.
carefrontation.usage: The expected mode of difficult conversations at TKE.

shared_fate.definition: Team members' success and failure are interconnected. Creates motivation for peer accountability.
shared_fate.usage: Core operating principle. Manifests through shared metrics and mutual accountability.

two_jobs: Model peer accountability — Have direct conversations yourself
Clear the organizational path — Remove blockers your team CAN'T solve
key_behaviors: Receive feedback publicly and thank the person
Own mistakes first — say 'I own that' before being asked
Track commitments visibly — same standard as team
Redirect escalation — 'Did you talk to them first?'
Clear the path — remove blockers team can't solve
Shield your team — absorb chaos from above so clarity flows down
Standards are standards — enforce consistently, no exceptions
48-hour response — respond to raised issues within 48 hours

agenda: Start with one win or thank you (gratitude)
Anyone have an example from yesterday? (reinforce culture)

standing_items: One recognition for living a fundamental
Leadership commitment check: 'What was promised? What was delivered?'

attributions.author: Eric Coryell
attributions.work: Revolutionize Teamwork: How to Create and Lead Accountable Teams
attributions.contribution: Shared fate, peer accountability, behavioral rules

attributions.author: Selena Reyes
attributions.contribution: Patient experience, 'Big Expertise. Small-Town Heart.' tagline, leadership specificity, compassionate accountability framing
